question: Make sure that the system URL address in your internet browser toolbar is correct and that it is the provincial system address. question: The error *Your access to the database is blocked* could be due to incorrect user ID and password. To reactivate access, contact the provincial office support and provide your national ID number.  The error "Your access to the database is blocked" can occur for one of the following reasons: 1. Error in entering the system URL address in the Internet browser bar: Make sure that the system URL address in your Internet browser bar is correct and that it is the provincial system address. 2. Wrongly entering the user ID and password more than three times: The user ID is your national number. The password is a combination of English numbers and letters. When entering the password, make sure that the language of your computer system is English and that the caps lock key is on or off to enter lowercase and uppercase English letters. 3. Wrong selection of the login panel: Note that users of municipalities/districts/service offices and surveyors must enter the system from the users section. Only supervising engineers can enter the system from the engineers section and citizens from the citizens section. 4. Failure to define a user or access authority in the system: Your user or access authority needs to be defined in the system. To investigate the problem, you need to complete form number .... and send it to the email address of the provincial support office.
answer: When logging into the system, I encountered the message "Your access to the database is blocked." What is the reason?

question: The error "User ID, password or one-time password is not valid" can occur for one of the following reasons: 1. Error in entering the system URL address in the Internet browser bar: Make sure that the system URL address in your Internet browser bar is correct and that the system address is provincial. . 2. Incorrect entry of user ID and password: The user ID is your national number. The password is a combination of English numbers and letters. When entering the password, make sure that the language of your computer system is English and that the caps lock key is on or off to enter lowercase and uppercase English letters. 3. Incorrect selection of the login panel: Note that users of municipalities/districts/service offices and surveyors must enter the system from the users section. Only supervising engineers can enter the system from the engineers section and citizens from the citizens section. 4. Password expiration: If you have received a temporary password, its validity period may have expired. From the password retrieval link, receive a new password again and enter the password in the system before its validity period expires. If you are still having trouble logging into the system, you need to complete form number .... and send it to the email address of the provincial support office.
answer: When logging into the system, I encountered the message "The user ID, password, or one-time code is not valid." What is the reason?
